sections.paragraph:
ἄτακτος, ον (s. ἀτακτέω) gener. ‘not in the proper order’ (3 Macc 1:19; TestNapht 2:9; Philo; Jos., Bell. 2, 517; 649, Ant. 15, 152; Ath. 25:3 [ἀταξία ibid. 3:2]; loanw. in rabb.)

① of volitional state, pert. to being out of step and going one’s own way, disorderly, insubordinate (Hdt., Thu. et al.; Sb 6152, 13; 6153, 16; PFlor 332, 4; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 32) 1 Th 5:14 (cp. SEG XXVII, 261 B, 99; s. CSpicq, Studia Theologica 10, ’56, 1–13); but some prefer the sense idle, indolent (s. ἀτακτέω and ἀτάκτως; also the orator Lycurgus 39 ἄτακτος=‘not at one’s post’).—GHolland, SBLSP 24, ’85, 327–41.

② of affective state pert. to being without socially recognized constraint, undisciplined φορά impulse Dg 9:1 (cp. Pla., Leg. 2 p. 660b ἄτακτοι ἡδοναί; Plut., Mor. 5a likew.).—M-M. TW. Spicq.

gener. gener. = generally

TestNapht TestNapht = Testament of Naphthali, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Lycurgus Lycurgus , IV b.c.—List 5

SBLSP SBLSP = Society of Biblical Literature Seminar Papers—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

likew. likew. = likewise

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 148
